details.body: `resolve_bead_plan` still breaks valid membership checks when the live bead ID and the stored plan ID use different milestone aliases. In [src/cli/project.rs:1914-1918](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:1914) and [src/cli/project.rs:2013-2025](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:2013), any entry with `bead_id` goes through raw string comparison only, and the `Some(_)` branch no longer reaches the title-fallback path. A live bead returned as `bead-2` / `8.5.3` will therefore fail against a plan entry stored as `ms-alpha.bead-2` / `9ni.8.5.3`, even though the codebase already treats those forms as equivalent elsewhere ([src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:3-17](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:3), [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3654-3670](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3654)). `project create-from-bead` can now reject a valid milestone/bead pair solely because `br show` returned the short form. Normalize both sides or reuse `milestone_bead_refs_match`, and add a regression test for short live IDs against qualified plan IDs.

The legacy-plan warning is only deduplicated per process, not per milestone across CLI invocations. [src/cli/project.rs:1880-1893](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:1880) stores warned milestones in a process-local `OnceLock<Mutex<BTreeSet<String>>>`, and [src/cli/project.rs:1967-1980](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:1967) calls it from `project create-from-bead`. Because each CLI invocation starts a fresh process, operators will see the same warning every time they run the command for the same legacy milestone. The bead explicitly requires a one-time warning per milestone, not per invocation, so this needs a persisted marker or another cross-invocation mechanism.

Affected: `src/cli/project.rs:1664-1725,1869-1872`. The branch adds alias-aware stable-ID matching (`milestone_bead_refs_match` / `canonicalize_milestone_bead_ref`), but the actual `project create-from-bead` entry path still treats any dotted bead ref as an exact string. `load_bead_detail` only retries `br show --no-db` for refs without dots, and `select_matching_bead_from_show_response` requires `bead.id == bead_id` whenever the query contains a dot. That means a valid short dotted alias like `8.5.3` will still be rejected if `br show` returns `9ni.8.5.3` (or vice versa), so the new alias support is not reachable end-to-end from the CLI. On top of that, `ensure_bead_belongs_to_milestone` still hard-checks `canonical_bead_id.starts_with("<milestone>.")`, which rejects the numeric-milestone alias case that `milestone_bead_refs_match` explicitly accepts. The lookup and membership checks need to use the same milestone-aware alias matcher for dotted refs, not the old exact-string gate.

finding_summary: `resolve_bead_plan` now marks every legacy title-only match as `membership_confirmed = false`, and `execute_create_from_bead_in_dir` uses that flag to skip `validate_milestone_plan_snapshot` and to drop `plan_hash`/`plan_version` from the created project. That means a supported pre-change plan with no `bead_id` can silently bypass stale `status.json` detection and lose plan lineage metadata even when the title fallback resolved a unique bead successfully.
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:373-392
- src/cli/project.rs:2055-2072
remediation_hint: keep the legacy warning path, but decouple it from snapshot/lineage validation. A unique successful legacy fallback should still validate the current plan snapshot and persist `plan_hash`/`plan_version`, or the code should introduce a separate flag for "used legacy fallback" instead of reusing `membership_confirmed` to disable those checks.

finding_summary: Short dotted alias resolution can select a bead from the wrong milestone. `milestone_bead_refs_match` treats `8.5.3` as equivalent to `9ni.8.5.3` / `10.8.5.3` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:3-49`), and `load_bead_detail`, `ensure_bead_belongs_to_milestone`, and `proposal_matches_bead_id` all trust that comparison (`src/cli/project.rs:1665-1755`, `src/cli/project.rs:1928-1937`, `src/cli/project.rs:2105-2113`). Another milestone with numeric id `8` can legitimately own bead `8.5.3`, so `project create-from-bead --milestone-id 10 --bead-id 8.5.3` can bind/claim the foreign bead instead of the planned `10.8.5.3`. remediation_hint: Do not treat arbitrary dotted ids as local aliases. Only accept a short dotted alias after resolving a bead that is proven to belong to the requested milestone (for example via the canonical `{milestone}.{alias}` form), and when the short-form lookup returns a foreign bead, continue to the canonical candidate instead of accepting or hard-failing immediately.

finding_summary: [src/cli/project.rs:1665-1763] `load_bead_detail()` now retries any qualified bead id with the stripped short form. That is required for canonicalized explicit ids such as `ms-explicit.ralph-burning-evi`, but it also applies to generated slot ids like `ms-alpha.bead-2`. If the exact bead is missing and an unrelated explicit bead `bead-2` exists, the fallback path accepts it via `milestone_bead_refs_match()`, so `project create-from-bead` can claim/bootstrap the wrong bead instead of failing. affected_files: src/cli/project.rs. remediation_hint: only strip-and-retry qualified ids that can represent authored explicit ids, or otherwise reject implicit-slot aliases like `bead-N` on the fallback path.

finding_summary: [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3650-3721] The new legacy title-only plan support is not durable for explicit beads. `resolve_bead_plan()` can now create a project from a legacy entry with no `bead_id`, but later lineage/history reads call `find_bead_plan_details()`, which only matches by stable `bead_id` or the implicit `bead-N` slot. For a legacy title-only entry that originally pointed at an explicit bead such as `api-1`, `read_bead_lineage()` / `bead_execution_history()` will fail with `bead 'api-1' was not found in the validated milestone plan` even when the stored `plan_hash` still matches. affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs. remediation_hint: persist enough metadata to re-identify legacy title-only matches, or make lineage lookup mirror the same legacy title fallback instead of hard-failing.

`bead_execution_history` now tries to recover stable lineage metadata by calling `find_matching_task_source_for_bead`, but that helper returns the first matching project from `list_project_ids()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3929-3949, 4004-4025`). `FsProjectStore::list_project_ids()` is lexicographically sorted, not ordered by recency or metadata quality (`src/adapters/fs.rs:1536-1575`), so when a bead has multiple terminal projects on the same `plan_hash`, an older project without `plan_workstream_index`/`plan_bead_index` can mask a newer indexed project. In that state, history/lineage reconstruction drops back to the legacy title-only path and can still lose bead title/acceptance data or fail on title drift even though a correct indexed task source exists. The helper needs deterministic selection that prefers indexed task sources (and ideally the newest matching project), plus a regression test with two same-bead projects sharing one plan hash.

finding_summary: Fully-qualified explicit bead lookups can now bind the wrong bead after the new short-form retry path. In `src/cli/project.rs:1728-1731`, a request like `ms-alpha.api-1` falls back to `api-1` when the qualified `br show` misses. That retry result is accepted by `bead_matches_show_query` (`src/cli/project.rs:1775-1784`) and then treated as local by `ensure_bead_belongs_to_milestone` (`src/cli/project.rs:1957-1973`) if the returned bead id is the unqualified `api-1`. In a workspace where another bead already owns that short explicit id, `project create-from-bead` can create/claim work for the foreign bead even though the requested fully-qualified bead was not found.

affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:1728-1731
- src/cli/project.rs:1775-1784
- src/cli/project.rs:1957-1973

remediation_hint: Do not short-retry fully-qualified explicit ids, or require the retry result to come back with an unambiguous same-milestone qualified id before accepting it.
